appendMonsterAbilities.name: Colossal
appendMonsterAbilities.rulesText: The titan doubles its STR bonus for the damage of melee attacks.

appendMonsterAbilities.name: Tunnel
appendMonsterAbilities.rulesText: The great worm can move to any zone on its current elevation level.

appendMonsterAbilities.name: Spirit Strike
appendMonsterAbilities.rulesText: The guardian beast deals bonus magic damage equal to its target's current MP.

appendMonsterAbilities.name: Healing Touch
appendMonsterAbilities.rulesText: Once per turn, the unicorn can restore a creature within its reach to full HP.

appendMonsterAbilities.name: Bewitch
appendMonsterAbilities.rulesText: The moon witch forces the target to immediately move, and controls one skill check on the target's next turn.

appendMonsterAbilities.name: Polymorph
appendMonsterAbilities.rulesText: The target is transformed into a frog until the end of its next turn. They can move, but cannot make skill checks or use magic. Their equipment is transformed with them.

appendMonsterAbilities.name: Binding Brambles
appendMonsterAbilities.rulesText: Whenever the dryad deals damage, the target is bound by thorny vines. They cannot move until they break free by scoring 8 on a STR check. While bound, the target takes 1 physical damage every time they make a skill check.

appendMonsters.name: Titan
appendMonsters.loreText: Guardians of the pillar worlds, the titans maintain cosmic order and the cycle of mortality.
notes: The titan has extra HP equal to its STR bonus.

appendMonsters.name: Kraken
appendMonsters.loreText: Horrors from the darkest depths, krakens strike fear into the hearts of seafarers everywhere. Their many tentacled arms slither silently through dark waters, dragging anything that moves into the abyss.

appendMonsters.name: Maelstrom Giant
appendMonsters.loreText: Maelstrom giants are the custodians of the aether, watching over the formless realm for all eternity. They are descended from Arxis, the primordial titan of light and darkness.

appendMonsters.name: Vulcan Giant
appendMonsters.loreText: Vulcan giants slumber among the mountains until their fiery rage is awoken, echoing across the land. They are descended from Proak, the primordial titan of earth and fire.

appendMonsters.name: Tempest Giant
appendMonsters.loreText: Tempest giants sail through the skies, bringing storms in their wake. They are descended from Vugol, the primordial titan of air and water.

appendMonsters.name: Phoenix
appendMonsters.loreText: Magical birds that soar on wings of flame, phoenixes represent rebirth.
notes: When the phoenix dies, it is reborn from its ashes after 24 hours.

appendMonsters.name: Sea Serpent
appendMonsters.loreText: Within the oceans of the Pillar Worlds lurk gargantuan creatures with voracious appetites. Sea serpents pose an enormous risk to any seafaring vessels that pass through the wrong waters.

appendMonsters.name: Great Worm
appendMonsters.loreText: Immense burrowing beasts, great worms consume the remains of magical beings and digest them into fertile earth.

appendMonsters.name: Air Spirit
appendMonsters.loreText: Air spirits carry the winds on their backs, heralding coming storms and the change of seasons.

appendMonsters.name: Fire Spirit
appendMonsters.loreText: Embodiments of pure flame, fire spirits may take many forms but are always dangerous.
notes: Many magi seek to tame fire spirits, harnessing their destructive energy in battle.
Fire spirits can be useful in domestic life if properly cared for.
Water is effective against them; starving them of fuel/oxygen is a good way to defeat them.

appendMonsters.name: Water Spirit
appendMonsters.loreText: Water spirits circulate life-giving energy throughout the pillar worlds. Their health is the health of the surrounding land, and a community that angers a water spirit risks drought or floods.

appendMonsters.name: Earth Spirit
appendMonsters.loreText: Earth spirits spend almost all of their time sleeping, and typically become a part of the surrounding environment.

appendMonsters.name: Aurean
appendMonsters.loreText: Aureans are minor air elementals that live amongst the clouds.

appendMonsters.name: Aquean
appendMonsters.loreText: Aqueans are minor water spirits that live in ponds and streams.

appendMonsters.name: Embrean
appendMonsters.loreText: Embreans are minor fire spirits that make their homes in lanterns, torches, and braziers.

appendMonsters.name: Terrean
appendMonsters.loreText: Terreans are minor earth elementals that live in caves, tunnels, and other subterranean places.

appendMonsters.name: Grundling
appendMonsters.loreText: Grundlings are ravenous little beasts that devour crops, ruin roads, and weaken the foundations of buildings.
notes: They can be dispelled by placing a salt circle around the affected region.

appendMonsters.name: Wilp
appendMonsters.loreText: Wilps are flying pests who swoop and claw at unprotected mortals, stealing any shiny items they see.
notes: They can be dispelled by sacrificing poultry.

appendMonsters.name: Znipe
appendMonsters.loreText: Znipes are little peeking shadows that curse mortals who meet their gaze.
notes: They can be dispelled by burning ceremonial herbs.

appendMonsters.name: Guardian Beast
appendMonsters.loreText: Fae creatures of immense spiritual power, guardian beasts defend sacred wilderness. Guardian beasts take many forms, some of which are descended from the zodiac beasts who guide the flow of mortal spirits.

appendMonsters.name: Unicorn
appendMonsters.loreText: Among the first fae creatures to wander Eden's mystical wilds, unicorns represent purity. They have unparalleled healing abilities, and are coveted for their magical horns.

appendMonsters.name: Moon Witch
appendMonsters.loreText: Fae magi of otherworldly beauty and grace, moon witches glow with the silver light of the night. They can enthrall mortals with a single glance, and are known to lure people into Eden.

appendMonsters.name: Cave Troll
appendMonsters.loreText: Cave trolls are the largest and toughest of their kind, thundering through deep caverns in search of fresh meat. They have keen noses that can pick up the scent of live prey from anywhere in their cave network.

appendMonsters.name: Savari
appendMonsters.loreText: Savari are vicious fae monsters who trick mortals into entering Eden to devour them. Savari were once Sylvani who betrayed their oaths to the noble zodiac beasts in favour of the dark fae.

appendMonsters.name: Woodland Troll
appendMonsters.loreText: Great lumbering brutes, woodland trolls love toying with mortals before gobbling them up. Moss and fungi grow from their back, allowing them to easily blend in with their surroundings.

appendMonsters.name: Green Witch
appendMonsters.loreText: Green witches reside in bogs, woodlands, and other places of dark wilderness. They are known for placing curses on mortals and spawning new monsters.

appendMonsters.name: Krikalith
appendMonsters.loreText: Born in the darkest recesses of Eden, krikaliths sneak into the material world during the new moon. They are tall, brittle creatures who disguise themselves as dead trees.

appendMonsters.name: Bridge Troll
appendMonsters.loreText: A troll is born when a goblin devours a fairy, absorbing its concentrated fae magic. Bridge trolls are the smallest of trolls, but can still easily gobble up most travelers.

appendMonsters.name: Werewolf
appendMonsters.loreText: Werewolves are shapeshifting monsters who adopt a humanoid form to lure vulnerable mortals away from safety.

appendMonsters.name: Satyr

equipment.name: Panflute
equipment.rulesText: The satyr gains advantage on its next skill check upon successful defense.

appendMonsters.loreText: Fae tricksters with horns and cloven hooves, satyrs spread revelry and mischief.

appendMonsters.name: Centaur
appendMonsters.loreText: Proud nomads who roam the fae realm of Eden, centaurs have the upper body of a human and the lower body of a horse.

appendMonsters.name: Boruk
appendMonsters.loreText: Furtive mountain monsters with curled horns, boruks have thick protective wool to survive in frigid conditions and strong arms for climbing steep crags.

appendMonsters.name: Krebble
appendMonsters.loreText: Small shelled monsters, krebbles hide among rock formations to ambush passing prey.

appendMonsters.name: Dryad
appendMonsters.loreText: Dryads are sentient plants that fortify and expand the wilderness.

appendMonsters.name: Fairy
appendMonsters.loreText: Fairies are unborn spirits that visit the material world to be incarnated as a new mortal life. They serve the wise zodiac beasts in the fae realm of Eden.

appendMonsters.name: Goblin
appendMonsters.loreText: Goblins are devious little tricksters who live to cheat, rob, and kill mortals. Goblins are corrupted fairies.
